title: Review migration and compatibility risks for adding first-class multi-reply citation support to x-auto. Focus on Notion schema limitations, backward compatibility with existing rows, scheduler safety, queue drift, and rollout strategy. Recommend the safest migration path and validation steps. Read-o...
summary_text: **Findings (Ordered by Risk)** 1. **Critical: Notion cannot round-trip first-class multi-reply citations today.** The Notion mapping reads/writes only a single URL field (`Source URLs` as Notion `url`), not a list, and does not parse/store `reply_url` in Notion at all. References: [notion_sync.py#L260](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L260), [notion_sync.py#L343](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L343), [notion_sync.py#L247](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L247). Impact: multi-citation data will drift/loss across Notion reloads unless explicitly dual-written and rehydrated. 2. **High: Runtime posting logic is string/newline-based, not structured.** Scheduler and manual publish build reply chains by splitting one string (`reply_url` or fallback `source_url`) by newline. References: [scheduler.py#L575](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L575), [scheduler.py#L634](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L634), [manual_publish.py#L83](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/manual_publish.py#L83), [manual_publish.py#L190](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/manual_publish.py#L190). Impact: introducing array/object citations without compatibility adapters will post malformed replies or skip replies. 3. **High: Scheduler marks post as `posted` before reply-chain completion.** Main tweet is committed and queue saved before reply-chain attempts; reply failures are non-critical and not retried. References: [scheduler.py#L624](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L624), [scheduler.py#L629](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L629), [scheduler.py#L631](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L631). Impact: multi-reply increases partial-chain probability, and current flow creates irreversible “posted-but-incomplete-citations” states. 4. **Medium: Queue audit/policy checks only legacy fields.** Audit checks citation presence and quote-reply policy via `source_url`/`reply_url` only, and note-post auto-fix clears only `reply_url`. References: [queue_audit.py#L82](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L82), [queue_audit.py#L253](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L253), [queue_audit.py#L356](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L356). Impact: first-class citation fields would bypass current safety checks unless audit is upgraded. 5. **Medium: Merge/supplement whitelists are hardcoded to legacy local-only keys.** Local supplement/preservation logic explicitly carries only `image_path` and `reply_url` from cache. References: [scheduler.py#L450](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L450), [post_generator.py#L283](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L283), [notion_sync.py#L446](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L446). Impact: new citation keys will silently disappear on Notion-first refresh unless included in all three paths. 6. **Medium: Upstream ingestion already encodes multi-citations as newline `reply_url`.** `citations[]` is flattened into newline text today; tests assert that contract. References: [scripts/apply_post_package.py#L73](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/apply_post_package.py#L73), [tests/test_apply_post_package.py#L56](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_apply_post_package.py#L56). Impact: changing canonical format abruptly will break current producer/consumer expectations. **Current queue signal**: `post_queue.json` has 29 approved posts, 25 with `source_url`, and 2 already using multiline `reply_url` (legacy encoding in active use). --- **Safest Migration Path** 1. Introduce an additive canonical field (example: `reply_citations: list[str]`) while keeping `source_url` + `reply_url` fully supported. 2. Add a single normalization function used by scheduler/manual/audit: `reply_citations` if present -> else split `reply_url` lines -> else fallback `[source_url]`. 3. Dual-write during rollout: keep writing legacy `reply_url` newline string plus new field locally. 4. Keep Notion `Source URLs` as the primary single URL for compatibility; store extra citation lines in a new No...
